title: Adult Swim NTE differences
wikitext:
[]: Adult Swim NTE differences?             Quote Post by AdamMalkovitch » Sat Dec 29, 2018 10:49 pm Does anyone know if there are any differences in the Adult Swim broadcasts of the New Theatrical Evangelion films? I know CN altered the first two episodes of Neon Genesis when they aired them during Giant Robot Week (although for the life of me I can't find any images or videos of said edits), but I'd be surprised if [AS] was able to air either series without any edits. I mean, I'm pretty sure you can't show a 14 year old's tiddies on American television...

[]: Re: Adult Swim NTE differences?             Quote Post by DarkBluePhoenix » Tue Jan 01, 2019 11:34 am I doubt there would be any differences, it's 2018 2019 and its Adult Swim, they have no need to edit anything down due to the time slot they broadcast in not having to be child friendly.

[]: Re: Adult Swim NTE differences?             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Wed Jan 02, 2019 12:03 pm I don't know. They've re-edited NTE before on Adult Swim. (At least, I think it was Adult Swim, and not Toonami.) Even if they're night-time programing, and even though the FCC has no jurisdiction over subscription-based media (such as cable network channels), a lot of networks still see it as good business to cater to the expectations of those who are used to broadcast television. I don't have cable, so I can't say either way if NTE was re-edited or not. But it wouldn't surprise me if it was, despite the legal freedoms Adult Swim has.

[]: Re: Adult Swim NTE differences?             Quote Post by C. Smith » Fri Jan 04, 2019 5:34 am FreakyFilmFan4ever wrote: I don't know. They've re-edited NTE before on Adult Swim. (At least, I think it was Adult Swim, and not Toonami.) Even if they're night-time programing, and even though the FCC has no jurisdiction over subscription-based media (such as cable network channels), a lot of networks still see it as good business to cater to the expectations of those who are used to broadcast television. I don't have cable, so I can't say either way if NTE was re-edited or not. But it wouldn't surprise me if it was, despite the legal freedoms Adult Swim has.
